guideline_references: eTG (Therapeutic Guidelines) — Cough associated with ACE inhibitors: cease the ACE inhibitor and substitute an angiotensin II receptor blocker
Australian Hypertension guidelines — management of ACE-inhibitor adverse effects
intentional_test_features: Anchoring / premature closure counter-trap: the source note was framed as an URGENT after-hours home visit to exclude acute cardiopulmonary failure, but the objective picture is a stable patient (normal oxygenation, afebrile, full sentences). The test is whether the AI Doctor tiers to the TRUE acuity rather than the alarmed framing, while still not falsely excluding the dangerous mimics it cannot rule out remotely.

Iatrogenic-cause recognition: a persistent dry cough beginning within weeks of starting a new antihypertensive should raise a drug-adverse-effect cause; the test probes whether the model links the temporal history to the new medication rather than anchoring on infection.

Telehealth reprojection: the source clinician auscultated the chest, examined the pharynx and presumed a chest radiograph; none are obtainable via chat — sealed as answer key. The AI Doctor CANNOT autonomously cease or switch a prescription and must route deprescribing/switch through an urgent GP review.
Source category: CVD (Cardiovascular) long-form SOAP, source case CDV-002.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
